biography: Mikael, representing Kurk Golf, made a singular, yet distinct, appearance in the Hector Trophée. This solitary outing offered a glimpse into his competitive faculties within the challenging and unique framework of this esteemed pairs competition.

During his one engagement with the tournament's rigorous multi-day format, which encompasses both individual contests and strategic pair play, Mikael applied himself with earnest commitment. However, despite his concerted efforts, neither the coveted Hector Trophée for the triumphant pair nor the prestigious Victor Trophy for individual supremacy came to fruition, highlighting the exacting competitive standard of the event.

With his involvement now concluded, Mikael is considered to have retired from active competition in Hector events. Consequently, he will not be among the distinguished field for the upcoming Hector Trophée 2026, bringing a quiet close to his chapter in this unique tournament's history.
